answer.text: Temporary suspension of trading on stock exchanges
Permanent removal of shares from being listed on a stock exchange
Transfer of listing from one exchange to another
Reduction in the face value of shares

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Delisting means permanent removal of a company's shares from the stock exchange listing. It can be compulsory (non-compliance with regulations) or voluntary (company buys back shares through reverse book building process). **The Trap:** Suspension of trading is temporary; delisting is permanent. These are very different corporate actions. **Cross-Exam Context:** Delisting is tested in X-A (Ch 6) and IX (Merchant Banking). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Voluntary delisting offers are usually at a premium — shareholders should evaluate the offer price against the company's fair value before tendering shares.
